# Runbook — ITS_Time_Off + the reviewer chain (PTO entry, chain skip logic, watchdog Check D gaps) (Successor-Remediation, Op Stds §43)

<!-- BEGIN TREE-XREF (generated by scripts/build_runbook_xrefs.py — do not edit inside) -->
> **In the troubleshooting tree** ([printable guide](../troubleshooting/troubleshooting_guide.md) · dashboard `/troubleshoot`). This runbook resolves:
>   - Safety report — portal submission to sent weekly packet → Below-confidence / flagged items route to human review → “A reviewer-chain assignment is missing or points at an out-of-office reviewer.”
<!-- END TREE-XREF -->

A §43 successor-remediation entry, written for the **Successor-Operator**: a trained
operator who runs Claude Code and reads Smartsheet rows + alert emails, but does **not**
read code. The §42 code-reader rationale lives in `shared/scheduling.py` (the PTO
fetcher + chain resolution) and `scripts/watchdog.py::_check_reviewer_chain_forward`
(Check D).

## Purpose

Human review is on the critical path of every workstream, so ITS tracks **who is out**
(the `ITS_Time_Off` sheet) and resolves a **reviewer chain** around them
automatically. This runbook explains how the sheet and the skip logic work, what a
watchdog **Check D** reviewer-chain gap looks like, and the low-class repair (fixing
time-off rows / confirming coverage) versus the escalation (changing the chain
roster itself).

## How it works

### The ITS_Time_Off sheet

`ITS_Time_Off` lives in the **ITS — Human Review** workspace ("06 — Personnel").
**One row per person per time-off span**, three cells the system reads:

- **Person** — the person, as a contact **with an email address**. The email is the
  join key to the reviewer chain; a display-name-only contact (no email behind it)
  cannot match anyone and the row is skipped with a WARN in ITS_Errors.
- **Start Date / End Date** — the span, **inclusive on both ends**. A single day off
  is a row with `Start Date = End Date`. Two separate absences are two rows.
- **Retroactive entries work**: a row added today for dates already past (or already
  underway) takes effect on the next lookup — useful when someone called in sick and
  the row is being entered after the fact.

If the sheet cannot be read at all, the system **fails open** ("nobody is out") and
logs a WARN — reviews keep flowing to the normal chain rather than everything halting.

### The reviewer chain and the skip logic

Each covered workstream has a three-tier chain — **primary, secondary, tertiary**
reviewer (each an email), plus two delay values (how many hours after an item lands
before the next tier is also brought in). Resolution is PTO-aware and **shifts up**:

- Anyone with a time-off row covering the date is **skipped automatically**.
- The remaining reviewers **promote into the vacant slots**: if the primary is out,
  the secondary becomes the immediate (0-hour) reviewer and the tertiary moves up one
  slot. No hand-off action is needed — entering the PTO row *is* the hand-off.
- If **every** configured reviewer is out on a date, the chain resolves **empty** for
  that date. Work is held for a human rather than auto-routed to anyone else — and
  that is exactly the gap Check D exists to catch *before* it happens.

### Watchdog Check D — the 14-day forward scan

Every morning the watchdog walks the next **14 days** for each covered workstream
(currently the safety-reports chain), skipping federal holidays, and resolves the
chain for each business day. Any day where the chain comes up **empty** is a **gap**.

Gaps are surfaced as an **anomaly row in ITS_Review_Queue** (one row per workstream
per run, Workstream `global`, Summary
`reviewer-chain gap detected (N day(s)) for workstream='…'`, with the exact gap dates
in the Payload). Check D does **not** deduplicate across runs — a gap left standing
produces a fresh row every morning until it is fixed, and watchdog **Check A** starts
WARNing once those rows sit PENDING past their SLA window. Growing daily rows = the
nag is working.

## Procedure

### Symptom

- ITS_Review_Queue rows with Summary starting `reviewer-chain gap detected`, naming a
  workstream and (in the Payload) the gap dates — i.e. days in the next two weeks
  where **no reviewer will be available**.
- Or, day-of: a review item is sitting unassigned/held and the expected reviewer is
  out.

### What the Successor-Operator checks / does (low-class)

1. **Open the Payload's gap dates** and `ITS_Time_Off`. For each gap date, look at the
   rows covering it — the gap means every chain member has (or appears to have) a
   time-off row spanning that day.
2. **Wrong or stale time-off data** → fix the rows (the canonical low-class repair):
   - A span entered too wide (wrong End Date) → correct the dates.
   - A cancelled/changed absence still on the sheet → delete or correct the row.
   - Someone's absence *missing* → add the row (Person **with email**, Start/End
     inclusive). Retroactive adds are fine.
   - A skipped-row WARN in ITS_Errors (`ITS_Time_Off row … skipped`) → re-enter the
     named row with a real email contact and parseable dates.
3. **The data is right and the gap is real** (everyone genuinely out) → this is a
   staffing question: confirm with the team whether one absence can move; the fix is
   still just editing time-off rows. If nobody can cover, tell Seth ahead of the gap
   window so the held work is expected.
4. After fixing, the next morning's Check D run confirms (no new gap row). Resolve
   the accumulated gap rows via the dashboard review/resolve verb
   (`docs/runbooks/review_queue_triage.md`) — Summary-prefix
   `reviewer-chain gap detected` is the stable filter.

> "Claude, Check D logged `reviewer-chain gap detected` for safety_reports with gap
> dates <dates>. Show me the ITS_Time_Off rows covering those dates so I can see whose
> entry is wrong or who needs one, and then help me resolve the gap rows."

### Escalate-to-Seth condition

- **Changing the chain itself** — who the primary/secondary/tertiary reviewers *are*,
  the escalation delays, or adding a workstream to the scan. The roster is
  configuration resolved in code-owned defaults (there is no operator-editable chain
  sheet), so a roster change is a **code change = FIXED high-class → Seth**. Editing
  `ITS_Time_Off` rows never requires this.
- A gap row for a workstream whose chain you believe is fully staffed and whose
  time-off rows look correct (a resolution/code fault — novel → Seth).
- Repeated `ITS_Time_Off fetch failed` WARNs in ITS_Errors (a Smartsheet auth/outage
  question — the fail-open means reviews still route, but the PTO skip logic is
  flying blind until the read recovers).

## Owner

`@solutionsmith`. If a second workstream's chain goes live, Check D's scan list and
this entry grow together; the empty-chain "hold + alert" semantics are doctrine and
stay put.
